question_text: A dolphin emits an ultrasound wave with a frequency of 150,000 Hz. The speed of sound in water is 1500 m/s. Calculate the wavelength of the ultrasound using the wave equation.
explanation: Using v = f × λ, rearrange to λ = v ÷ f = 1500 ÷ 150,000 = 0.01 m. This demonstrates that ultrasound has very short wavelengths, which is why it is useful for detailed medical imaging.
